generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE contract syntax and type errors, not modifying the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helper variables in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) are used correctly for constants and equations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Range checks are often expressed as `eq range_name : bool = x >= lower and x <= upper;`
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on fixing the contract itself.
Avoid placing guarantee statements within implementation annexes.
Do not attempt to resolve type errors by changing the AADL model.
